lede: A look at July 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alex Consani and David Strettle, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2018 Attica wildfires
events.desc: A wildfire in East Attica kills at least 102 people. It is the deadliest wildfire in the history of Greece.

events.title: One Direction
events.desc: The English-Irish boy band One Direction were formed while auditioning for the 2010 series of the British singing competition The X Factor.

events.title: Megawati Sukarnoputri
events.desc: Megawati Sukarnoputri was sworn in as the first female president of Indonesia following her predecessor's impeachment.

events.title: Comet Hale–Bopp
events.desc: Comet Hale–Bopp is discovered; it becomes visible to the naked eye on Earth nearly a year later.

events.title: Catholic Church
events.desc: A Vatican commission, led by Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger, establishes that limiting certain rights of homosexual people and non-married couples is not equivalent to discrimination on grounds of race or gender.

events.title: Glenville shootout
events.desc: Glenville shootout: In Cleveland, Ohio, a violent shootout between a Black Militant organization and the Cleveland Police Department occurs. During the shootout, a riot begins and lasts for five days.

events.title: 1967 Detroit riot
events.desc: Detroit Riots: In Detroit, one of the worst riots in United States history begins on 12th Street in the predominantly African American inner city. It ultimately kills 43 people, injures 342 and burns about 1,400 buildings.

events.title: United States Under Secretary of State
events.desc: The United States' Under Secretary of State Sumner Welles issues a declaration on the U.S. non-recognition policy of the Soviet annexation and incorporation of three Baltic states: Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania.

events.title: Boundary Treaty of 1881 between Chile and Argentina
events.desc: The Boundary Treaty of 1881 between Chile and Argentina is signed in Buenos Aires.

events.title: Knights Hospitaller
events.desc: A Knights Hospitaller fleet scores a crushing victory over an Aydinid fleet off Chios.

births.name: Georges V. Matchabelli
births.line: Georgian-American businessman, created Prince Matchabelli perfume (died 1935)

deaths.text: Robin Warren, Australian pathologist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Eddie Rickenbacker, American pilot and race car driver, founded Rickenbacker Motors

observances.text: Christian feast day: Liborius of Le Mans
Christian feast day: Mercè Prat i Prat
